messages.subject: [R-gui] IDE/Script Editor
messages.body_plain:
Dear Vytautas,

I've taken a look at this editor, and it seems very nice. I've also looked
quickly at the documentation, but wasn't able to figure out whether it's
possible to run the syn editor with an interactive R session, sending
selected text to be executed (in the manner of WinEdt or ESS). Is that
possible? If so, can you explain briefly how it's done?

Thanks,
 John

On Mon, 30 Jun 2003, Vytautas M. wrote:

> take a look at
>
> syn.sf.net
>
> for open source, customisable syn Text Editor with R syntax highliting.
>
> Vytautas Maniusis
>
> _______________________________________________
> R-SIG-GUI mailing list
> R-SIG-GUI@stat.math.ethz.ch
> https://www.stat.math.ethz.ch/mailman/listinfo/r-sig-gui
>
>

messages.subject: [R-gui] IDE/Script Editor
messages.body_plain:
Dear John,

I'm trying to find out this. I'm not the developer of syn and there is lack 
of documentation.
What I've found is:
One can attach programs, batch commands and active scripts(VB, JavaScript, 
Perl, Python etc)
to a toolbar buttons by
Tools>Configure>...
or
Run>Run File>Configure...

At least I've done this with latex and Acrobat Reader

Vytautas Maniusis,
Vilnius University, Lithuania

messages.subject: [R-gui] IDE/Script Editor
messages.body_plain:
Dear Vytautus,

Thanks for the additional information. I figured out how to run a program,
and it's clear that the editor is set up to interact with a compiler or
interpreter, processing a source file (or files) and then terminating, but
it was unclear to me whether it is possible to communicate with an active
R process.

Regards,
 John

and it's clear that the editor is set up to interact with a compiler or
interpreter, processing a source file

messages.subject: [R-gui] IDE/Script Editor
messages.body_plain:
Dear John,

Solution I've found applies not only for syn:
create file named, for example, "Rscript.vbs" (file extension must be vbs - 
for visual basic scripting),
containing:

Dim WshShell, oExec
Set WshShell = CreateObject("WScript.Shell")
Set oExec = WshShell.Exec("C:\Program Files\R\rw1070\bin\rgui.exe")
WshShell.AppActivate "Calculator"
WScript.Sleep 100
WshShell.SendKeys "x<-1:10{ENTER}"

save it and doubleclick in windows explorer

see more for Windows Scripting Host at http://msdn.microsoft.com/scripting/

Vytautas

messages.subject: [R-gui] tcltk: "Image" rather than bitmap loaded on tklabel
messages.subject_clean: [R-gui] tcltk: "Image" rather than bitmap loaded on tklabel
messages.body_plain:
Hi all,

How can I display images from external files in tcltk?

In Perl/Tk, I can use the Tk::Photo widget to load a .png graphic file,
which then goes into a label or button widget. In R, I only managed to do
that with a bitmap which I converted from a .png file. 

Is there a simple way without having to re-code pieces of, say, tkrplot?

Thanks, Dirk


-- 
Don't drink and derive. Alcohol and analysis don't mix.

messages.subject: [R-gui] Re: tcltk: "Image" rather than bitmap loaded on tklabel
messages.subject_clean: [R-gui] Re: tcltk: "Image" rather than bitmap loaded on tklabel
messages.body_plain:
Dirk,

Did you figure out how to display images in Tk widgets?

I finally got it working as follows:

library(tcltk)
tt <- tktoplevel()
tclRequire("Img")
tkcmd("image","create","photo","foo",file="foo.png")
imgAsLabel <- tklabel(tt,image="foo",bg="white")
tkpack(imgAsLabel)

(For a while I was completely missing the point that the image 
was supposed to go in a Tk label widget!)

Regards,
James

> How can I display images from external files in tcltk?
> Is there a simple way without having to re-code pieces of, say, tkrplot?
> Thanks, Dirk

messages.subject: [R-gui] Re: tcltk: "Image" rather than bitmap loaded on tklabel
messages.subject_clean: [R-gui] Re: tcltk: "Image" rather than bitmap loaded on tklabel
messages.body_plain:
James Wettenhall <wettenhall@wehi.edu.au> writes:

> Dirk,
> 
> Did you figure out how to display images in Tk widgets?
> 
> I finally got it working as follows:
> 
> library(tcltk)
> tt <- tktoplevel()
> tclRequire("Img")
  ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

Ah, that was what you needed to get PNG. However, note that the Img
extension isn't installed by default, and is not included with e.g.
RedHat. Without it, you only get GIF and PNM support (but of course
nearly everything can be converted to PNM).

> tkcmd("image","create","photo","foo",file="foo.png")
> imgAsLabel <- tklabel(tt,image="foo",bg="white")
> tkpack(imgAsLabel)

1.8.0 should have a tkimage.create function that actually works, i.e.

  tkimage.create("photo", img, file="foo.pnm")

should work. 

Already in the current version, you can use a tclVar object instead of
an explicit image name. This avoids trouble if two functions use the
same name, or accidentally overwriting an existing Tcl command (e.g.
calling the image "image" is a bad idea.) The finalizer code is
probably underdeveloped, though: An image should be deleted with
"image delete foo", not "unset foo", so it is not getting cleared
automatically when an image variable goes out of scope. 

-- 
   O__  ---- Peter Dalgaard             Blegdamsvej 3  
  c/ /'_ --- Dept. of Biostatistics     2200 Cph. N   
 (*) \(*) -- University of Copenhagen   Denmark      Ph: (+45) 35327918
